title: LME Aluminium Price Surge Hits $3,269/Tonne in March 2026
summary: LME aluminium prices reached $3,269 per tonne amid inventory drawdowns and supply constraints. The surge reflects ongoing market fundamentals pressuring the aluminium sector in early 2026.

title: Aluminum Mar '26 (ALH26) - Futures - Barchart.com
summary: Aluminum futures for March 2026 hit a period high of $3,464 on March 12, up 14.18% over the past month and 27.40% over 52 weeks. Commitment of Traders data shows shifts in commercial and non-commercial positions as of March 3.

title: Global Aluminium Industry Outlook 2026
summary: The video discusses 2026 aluminium demand outlook, bauxite, alumina, and primary aluminium trends, plus impacts from Middle East tensions on energy and supply. Pricing scenarios project averages of $3,400-$3,600 short-term and $3,150-$3,250 annually, with potential metal shortages.

title: Aluminum price drops as Trump moves to narrow levies on metal products
summary: Aluminum prices fell 0.7% to $3,077.50 per ton on the LME amid reports of potential US tariff rollbacks on derivative products. This follows Trump's June doubling of tariffs to 50%, which had disrupted markets and raised prices.

title: Metal Market Update: Steel & Aluminum Prices Heading Into 2026
summary: Aluminum markets face high Midwest premiums up 400% due to tariffs and supply constraints into 2026. Discussion covers LME vs. Midwest pricing deltas, potential imports, and new domestic capacity expectations.

title: US aluminium prices break away as tariffs collide with global supply strain
summary: US aluminium prices exceeded $5,000 per tonne due to tariffs, falling imports, shrinking stocks, and Europe's supply disruptions. Global supply strains are exacerbating the tight market conditions.

title: Century Aluminum amps up American aluminum production in South Carolina
summary: Century Aluminum is boosting U.S. aluminum production in South Carolina, supported by Trump-era tariffs. Fox Business correspondent Lydia Hu reports on the expansion efforts amid efforts to increase domestic supply. The segment aired on 'Mornings with Maria.'

title: Global Aluminium Industry Outlook 2026
summary: This webinar discusses the global aluminium demand outlook for 2026, including bauxite, alumina, and primary aluminium market trends. It covers new smelting capacity additions of 10-12 million tons outside China, impacts from Middle East tensions, and production updates like Balco's 435,000-ton expansion. Topics also include pricing, market balance, downstream demand, and recycling challenges.

title: The Future Of Aluminum: Trends And Innovations in 2026
summary: The article discusses high-end alloy innovations and precision forming technologies using nano-reinforced molds to extend service life and reduce costs. It emphasizes green transformation through circular economy practices, including clean energy adoption reducing carbon footprint by 22% per ton, direct aluminum liquid supply cutting energy use by 15%, and 95% scrap recycling. Modular profiles and eco-friendly finishes support 100% recyclability and reduced chemical waste.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026: Pioneering Sustainability and Innovation
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 trade fair in Düsseldorf, Germany, from October 6–8 will focus on sustainability, innovation, and collaboration in the aluminum industry. It highlights secondary aluminum's role in decarbonization, advancements in recycling, casting, automation, and surface finishing for sectors like mobility and construction. New features include Hall 7A for semi-finished products, expanded conferences on sustainability and recycling.

title: ALUMINIUM 2026 Launches New Industry Innovation Awards
summary: ALUMINIUM 2026 introduces Innovation Awards in categories: Sustainable & Low-Carbon Manufacturing, Technology & Product Innovation, and Smart Manufacturing, Automation & Connectivity. Exhibitors submit entries from May, with community voting in September and winners announced at the event. The awards aim to celebrate and promote breakthroughs to raise industry standards and attract policymaker attention.

title: Why Is Aluminum Scrap Suddenly Shaping the Future? The 2026 Global Shockwave Explained
summary: The aluminum scrap market is booming due to policy shifts and demand for recycled materials in construction, automotive, and electronics sectors. Asia-Pacific leads with innovations in closed-loop recycling, green smelting, and advanced sorting; recycled aluminum uses 95% less energy than primary production. This positions scrap as key to circular economy and climate goals amid market volatility.

title: United States Aluminium Extrusion Market 2026 | Growth Drivers
summary: In January 2026, Japanese producers invested in energy-efficient, low-emission technologies, while U.S. extruders focused on efficiency amid rising costs. December 2025 saw acceleration in automation, digitalization for precision and waste reduction, and expanded use of lightweight extrusions in EVs, aerospace, and green buildings. These trends integrate Industry 4.0 and support sustainability in extrusion.

title: Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Steel fell to 3,134 CNY/T on March 13, 2026, down 0.13% from the previous day, though up 3.33% over the past month. Steel rebar futures climbed above CNY 3,140 per ton, hitting six-week highs amid China's pledges to curb excess capacity. Production cuts in northern steel hubs were ordered to limit emissions during political meetings.

title: HRC Steel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: HRC Steel rose to 1,065 USD/T on March 13, 2026, up 0.28% from the previous day, with a 6.85% monthly gain and 11.29% yearly increase. It recently hit a 25-month high of 996 USD/T. The market shows upward momentum despite daily fluctuations.

title: Major steel and raw material futures prices in China - March 13, 2026
summary: Rebar futures at Shanghai Futures Exchange closed at RMB 3,142/mt ($455/mt), up 0.58%. Weekly changes include iron ore up 5.18%, rebar FOB Turkey up 2.26%, and HRC FOB China up 2.11%. Turkish merchant bar export prices trended up amid higher energy costs.

title: Steel prices in the third week of March 2025 - Arab Iron and Steel
summary: Scrap prices rose to $380/tonne, up $8 from last week, and iron ore to $104/tonne, up $4. Billet prices held at 435–465 USD/tonne, while rebar increased to 530–585 USD/tonne from 520–575 last week.

title: Border States Commodity Update — March 2026
summary: 3/4-inch steel pipe prices rose 7-10% nationally due to higher demand from data centers and spring construction. Price hikes aim to control demand and build inventory. Broader metals market affected by Middle East tensions.

title: Copper Price Falls for Three Consecutive Days
summary: Copper prices fell 1.89% to US$5.76 per pound, marking three consecutive days of declines. This drop is higher than the average weekly movement, with year-to-date growth at 0.02%. The price is down 7.2% from this year's high of US$6.2 per pound.

title: Copper - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Copper fell to 5.63 USD/Lbs on March 13, 2026, down 3.36% from the previous day. Over the past month, prices have fallen 2.32%, though still up 15.87% year-over-year. Historical high was 6.58 in January 2026.

title: Copper Pressured by Dollar Strength
summary: Copper futures fell below $5.8 per pound on Friday, marking a third consecutive session of losses. The decline was driven by a strengthening dollar.

title: BC Copper 2604 Closed Lower with a Wide Trading Range
summary: BC copper prices slid straight down during the daytime session, dipping to 88,550 yuan/mt near the close before settling lower. The wide trading range was pressured by geopolitics and interest rate cut expectations.

title: March 2026 Weekly Copper Report: Prices Surpass $11000 Amid AI Infrastructure Surge And Supply Constraints
summary: Copper prices rallied to $11,280 per metric ton amid AI data center expansion and supply disruptions from Chilean and Peruvian mines. LME stocks hit 14-month lows, supporting prices above $11,000 despite global borrowing costs. The report highlights decoupling from traditional cycles due to tech demand.

title: Refined copper output growth to slow in 2026: ICSG
summary: Refined copper output growth is forecasted to slow to 0.9% in 2026 from 3.4% in 2025, with a 150,000-metric ton market deficit expected. World mine production rises 2.3%, while refined usage grows 2.1%. Figures align with prior ICSG forecasts.

title: Viewpoint: US copper faces uncertain 2026 outlook
summary: US copper prices remain volatile in 2026 due to uncertain Chinese demand, supply shortages from lack of new mines, and slipping South American output. Record US inventories may require additional foreign production to meet demand. Forecasts vary, with Citigroup predicting over $5.90/lb by Q2 2026.

title: Ten major mining tech trends in 2026: Part 2
summary: The article discusses advancements in copper mining technology, including coarse particle flotation and new leaching methods for low-grade sulphide ores to boost global copper supply. BHP’s chief development officer highlights potential uplifts from processing marginal ores, while Metso focuses on coarse ore grinding and beneficiation to reduce grinding needs. These innovations aim to address declining ore grades in major deposits like those in Chile and Peru.

title: Ten major mining tech trends in 2026: Part 1
summary: Freeport-McMoRan is targeting production of 360,000 tons per annum of copper by reprocessing sulphide waste using new leaching technologies, unlocking value from stockpiles. Atlantic Copper’s CirCular project will produce copper and other metals from e-waste, launching this quarter. The piece also emphasizes water-saving innovations that could reduce consumption per tonne of copper by up to 78%, promoting sustainability.

title: Copper in the Age of AI: Challenges of Electrification
summary: Copper demand is surging due to AI data centers, electrification, EVs, and renewables, with Asia leading growth and global electricity demand rising 2.7% annually through 2040. Solutions include accelerating mine development with new technologies, expanding processing capacity, and incentivizing innovation to meet supply challenges. The report stresses the need for stable investment frameworks to avoid vulnerabilities.

title: Copper demand rockets in the Age of AI
summary: Global copper demand is exploding from AI, EVs, renewable energy, and defense, pushing consumption far beyond current supply capabilities. The article highlights the critical role of copper in these sustainable technologies amid supply shortages. Innovations in mining and processing are essential to sustain this growth.

title: 3-Month Nickel Commodity Price Falls to US$17,530/Ton Level (Friday, March 13, 2026)
summary: The 3-month forward nickel commodity price dropped to US$17,530 per ton on Friday, March 13, 2026, down from US$17,725 the previous day. Over the past week, prices fell 0.26% with an average of US$17,480 per ton, though year-to-date in 2026, prices have risen 3.64%. The article notes a five-year annual downward trend but monthly rises, with Indonesia's nickel exports projected to reach US$60 billion in 2025 due to EV downstreaming.

title: Nickel - Price - Chart - Historical Data - News
summary: Nickel prices fell to 17,320 USD/T on March 13, 2026, down 2.50% from the previous day, amid a broader pullback in industrial metals. Factors include rising Middle East tensions, surging Brent crude, and a stronger dollar prompting investors to reduce exposure to cyclical metals. Monthly gains stand at 1.05%, with year-over-year increases of 4.65%.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Nickel prices dropped 1.98% to $17,278.27/ton globally due to a stronger US dollar, Middle East tensions like threats to the Strait of Hormuz, and ongoing oversupply. Despite this, demand from China stainless steel and EV sectors provides some support, while Indonesia hints at a 34% output cut and mine halts add caution. Analysts forecast prices to settle between $15,000-$16,000 per tonne amid surpluses.

title: METALMARKETUPDATE - MARCH 2026
summary: Nickel futures fell to around $7.801 per pound in early March due to Middle East geopolitical tensions, including Iran's Strait of Hormuz threat, boosting oil prices and strengthening the dollar. Indonesia's 2026 ore quota at 270 million wet metric tons and anti-illegal mining efforts limit supply tightness, while new projects like PT Vale’s Pomalaa provide steady feedstock.

title: Nickel Prices Jump After Top Producer Indonesia Signals 2026 Output Cut
summary: Indonesia plans to cut nickel production in 2026 to balance supply and demand, as announced by Energy and Mineral Resources Minister Bahlil Lahadalia, amid rising LME inventories. Nickel prices surged to $16,500 per tonne following the announcement, with the government adjusting production quotas for miners. The move aims to reduce surplus, support prices, and limit environmental impact, similar to actions taken by the DRC with cobalt.

title: World's Biggest Nickel Mine in Indonesia Told to Slash Output | The Pulse
summary: The world's largest nickel mine, PT Weda Bay Nickel in Indonesia, has been ordered to cut ore production quota to 12 million tons this year from 42 million tons in 2025 to boost global nickel prices. The company plans to seek a revision of the quota. Nickel prices climbed last month after Indonesia signaled output cuts, impacting its key export used in stainless steel and EV batteries.

title: Indonesia quota talk triggers nickel reality check
summary: Reports of Indonesia slashing 2026 nickel ore production quotas by about a third to 250 million tonnes from 379 million in 2025 triggered a nickel price surge to $18,524 per tonne on the LME. Analysts note this could remove 700,000 tonnes of supply, potentially shifting the market to deficit, though processing capacity bottlenecks outside China limit quick responses. Skeptics warn the market is pricing speculation rather than confirmed action.

title: Indonesian industry players warn against disrupting nickel ecosystem under US trade pact
summary: The Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry has set Indonesia's 2026 nickel ore production quota at 260-270 million tonnes. Industry players caution against disruptions to the nickel ecosystem amid US trade pact considerations.

title: During the night session on January 14, 2026, nickel prices on the ...
summary: Nickel prices surged with LME 3M at $18,785/mt (up 6.73%) and SHFE 2602 hitting 151,750 yuan/mt, driven by supply concerns from Indonesia's quota cuts. However, spot trading remains sluggish with weak end-user demand and dropping premiums. Medium-term prices hinge on Indonesia's policy execution and demand exceeding expectations amid high inventories.

title: Indonesia cuts 2026 nickel quota, global prices jump immediately
summary: Indonesia's cut to the 2026 nickel production quota has sparked supply concerns, immediately driving up global nickel prices.

title: Re-energising the nickel industry through sustainable battery production
summary: Queensland Pacific Metals is developing the TECH Project to become a leading supplier of high-grade, ethically derived nickel for EV batteries using the patented DNi Process™. The project imports nickel laterite ore from New Caledonia to a carbon-negative facility in Townsville, Australia, with offtake agreements from LG Energy Solution and POSCO. This supports sustainable production of critical battery metals amid the green energy transition.

title: Nickel Prices Hit $18,000 in 2026 Amid Global Oversupply, US Boosts Domestic Supply Chain
summary: Nickel prices are projected to reach $18,000 in 2026 despite global oversupply, driven by surging demand for battery-grade nickel tripling by 2030 for high-performance EVs. Alaska Energy Metals' Nikolai Project, featuring the largest US nickel resource at Eureka, advances via FAST-41 permitting to strengthen domestic supply chains. The project aligns with US efforts to reshore critical minerals for energy transition and national security.

title: SEEC Innovation and Technology Task Force Endorses Bills to Bolster American Innovation in Clean Technologies
summary: The House SEEC Innovation and Technology Task Force endorsed four bills to accelerate deployment of next-generation clean technologies, emphasizing US leadership in innovation for energy independence. The initiative addresses scaling breakthroughs in clean tech to counter foreign competition and combat climate challenges. It builds on principles to boost federal investments in manufacturing and research.

title: Indonesia Nickel Processing Utilization Drops 15-20%
summary: Indonesia's nickel processing utilization has dropped 15-20%, with HPAL plants facing challenges in producing battery-grade nickel sulphate via pressure leaching technology. This impacts efforts to advance nickel for sustainable battery applications amid global supply dynamics.

title: Tenaris to invest in hydrogen steelmaking in Italy
summary: Tubemaker Tenaris has signed an agreement with Italian energy suppliers Edison and Snam for a project using hydrogen in steelmaking at the Tenaris Dalmine seamless tube plant in Italy. The initiative focuses on studying hydrogen supply and adapting the mill for its use, potentially lowering CO2 emissions further as part of the Dalmine Zero Emissions project. After initial tests, further hydrogen applications will be evaluated.

title: Informazione Regolamentata n. 0602-7-2026 - Weekly Report on Tenaris Share Buyback Program
summary: Tenaris S.A. released its weekly report for February 23-27, 2026, on the second tranche of its USD1.2 billion share buyback program, detailing share repurchases on the MTA trading venue. The report includes specifics on shares purchased, weighted average prices in EUR, and conversions to USD. This update is part of Tenaris' ongoing buyback efforts announced in November 2025.
